title: auto_learn.rs: prune_old_data uses string-interpolated table names in SQL — technically safe but bypasses parameterization for future-safety
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Security and Access / KISS. Location: src/auto_learn.rs:98-120 (prune_old_data). Violation: The four tables are hardcoded string constants so this is currently not a live injection surface, but the pattern — format!'ing SQL with {table} and {max_rows} — sets a precedent. If a future contributor passes a computed table name or a config-derived limit, it silently becomes an injection. The MAX_ROWS values are also not parameterized; they're formatted into SQL. execute_raw is called rather than a prepared statement. Mandated Refactor: Replace execute_raw with a prepared statement keyed by table enum. Use sqlite bound parameters for max_rows. Prohibit constructing SQL via string interpolation in the auto_learn module — add a lint/comment at module top.
